core_payload.content:
# Background Agent Runtime Daemon Lifecycle

## 1. Boundary
Background Agent Runtime Daemon Lifecycle owns the host-facing life of long-lived agent execution: daemon supervision, shift windows, ignition, model-access readiness, cost posture, and off-peak compute discipline. This hub exists so runtime bring-up and runtime continuation stay separate from policy gates and from work payload orchestration.

## 2. What Belongs Here
This sub-hub carries the atoms that keep the runtime alive and economically sane: `agent-daemon`, `night-shift-autonomy`, `ignition-ritual`, `ai-wallet`, `hybrid-llm-access`, `ai-economics-night-batch`, and `sleep-time-compute`. Together they define whether the background runtime can start, what supply it can draw from, how unattended windows are shaped, and how host lifecycle remains visible and recoverable.

## 3. What Does Not Belong Here
This sub-hub does not own mutation permission, workspace profiling, branch review, deep-intake posture, assignment continuity, or job execution routing. Those concerns belong to `mutation-gates`, `control-plane`, and `work-orchestration` so daemon survival never silently becomes policy authority.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Background Agent Runtime Daemon Lifecycle is the host-facing sub-hub of the runtime. It groups daemon supervision, ignition, off-peak posture, model-access readiness, wallet-governed supply, and runtime economics so lifecycle law stays separate from mutation permission and job orchestration. This boundary matters because unattended runtime survival should have one explicit place where host bring-up, cost posture, and model readiness are governed together rather than leaking into control-plane continuity or work-execution logic. It gives the runtime one stable seam for staying alive without silently becoming policy authority.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted as part of background-agent-runtime hub decomposition to reduce parent link density from 44 outbound to ≤15.
Expanded the daemon-lifecycle summary to satisfy validator length requirements and normalized the semantic hash to an eight-token canonical form while preserving the existing lifecycle boundary.
